exampleSentencesTranslated: I often hang out with uncle on weekends when I am free.
My uncle lives in another city so I rarely see him.
Yesterday, uncle gave me a very interesting book.

translation.en: I see the mouse on the tree.

example.vi: Con chu đang chơi trong vườn.
example.es: El ratón está jugando en el jardín.
example.en: The mouse is playing in the garden.

definitions.es: Un pequeño roedor que generalmente tiene una cola larga y es conocido por su capacidad de reproducirse rápidamente.
definitions.vi: Một loài gặm nhấm nhỏ thường có đuôi dài và nổi tiếng với khả năng sinh sản nhanh.
definitions.en: A small rodent that typically has a long tail and is known for its ability to reproduce quickly.

notes:
Chú = your father’s younger brother (uncle on your dad’s side).  

It’s a noun: say một người chú, hai người chú; in family talk, you often hear chú + name.  

Very common in speech: you use chú to address a man older than you but younger than your parents (like “uncle” in English, but broader).  

It can also be a pronoun: an older man may call himself chú when talking to a child.  

Compare: bác (parent’s older sibling) — older than your parent, while chú is younger than your father.
exampleSentences: Em thường đi chơi với chu vào cuối tuần khi rảnh.
Chu của anh sống ở thành phố khác nên ít khi gặp.
Hôm qua, chu tặng cho em một cuốn sách rất hay.

translation.en: I see the mouse on the tree.

notes_md:
**Chú** = your father’s younger brother (uncle on your dad’s side).  

It’s a noun: say **một người chú**, **hai người chú**; in family talk, you often hear **chú + name**.  

Very common in speech: you use **chú** to address a man older than you but younger than your parents (like “uncle” in English, but broader).  

It can also be a pronoun: an older man may call himself **chú** when talking to a child.  

Compare: **bác** (parent’s older sibling) — older than your parent, while **chú** is younger than your father.
